explanation: Incorrect vecEditMode argument; should use TA::VTT instead of TA::VECD.
Incorrect voltage unit and pin name, and uses .burst() instead of .execute()
Premature RDI_END() terminates the RDI context before the I2C write is executed.
Uses readHumanSeniority/getHumanSeniority instead of the correct readHumSensor/getHumSensor.
Using read() instead of execute() for the Pin2 waveform step, causing the intended command not to be executed.
The call incorrectly targets `rdi.burstUpload.smartVec()`; it should invoke `rdi.smartVec().burstUpload().end()` to properly close the burst upload.
The `iClamp` call uses the limits in the wrong order (`iClamp(50 mA, -50 mA)`) which produces an invalid clamp range; it should be `iClamp(-50 mA, 50 mA)` as in the correct code.
Calling a non‑existent method burst on a vector; should clear the vector instead.
Should use getFFV instead of getFFC to retrieve the value.

Incorrect use of `imeas().write()` with wrong pin selection and missing `execute()`; should call `iMeas().execute()` on the proper pins ("DPS_1,DPS_2") instead of `.imeas().write()` on ("DPS_0,DPS_1").
Incorrect pin identifier "D0"; should be "DO" to correctly reference the desired channel.
Missing argument "AVI" in getAlarmValue call.
The line incorrectly calls `.end().end()` instead of the proper method chain `.node().end()`, leading to an extra `end()` call and wrong behavior.
getVesjkctor is a typo in the method call; it should be getVector.
Passing an unexpected argument 70 to readTempThresh when it takes no parameters.
retrievePmuxPinStatus is called before RDI_END, but it must be executed after ending the RDI session.
vForceRange value should be 30 V, not 35 V
The argument should be true to enable the burst site upload; using false disables it, causing incorrect behavior.
Wrong method name iMeans and incorrect unit mAh; should use iMeas and mA.
Incorrect voltage setting (31 V) should be 30 V to match the default voltage.
